title: Isolate migration modes and tell the truth before the first renamed release
action: Implement D9 as a bounded final correction. In src/main.rs, route the parsed Args through one main-called migration decision seam and table-test: default and bind-only override DO migrate; doctor, doctor combined with other flags, demo, explicit config, and explicit-config+demo DO NOT move default legacy config/data. The tests must prove wiring from the same mode predicate main calls, not only an isolated unused helper; use temp roots/injected operations rather than process-global HOME races. Preserve explicit-config storage semantics beyond suppressing legacy-default moves. Give dev-deploy.sh the same platform/tool/systemctl user-manager preflight-before-mutation discipline as install.sh. Extend the contract test to enumerate executed mutation classes after preflight: migrate calls, npm bundle, cargo compile, directory/file/unit writes, disable/remove/reload/enable/restart; use fake command/PATH failure coverage where practical so failed prerequisites leave legacy directories untouched, and do not mistake function definitions for executed mutations. Update README/install/usage/specs to state truthfully that curl quick install requires a published matching herdr-go asset and is currently pending the first renamed release; provide truthful source/development alternatives. Add an explicit release-checklist trigger saying this caveat is removed only after asset presence plus real download/extract/run/service smoke evidence. Fix only the stale release workflow comment about separate static assets; keep the bundle step because UI embedding needs it. Extend tests/rename_contract.sh to enforce mode isolation, dev preflight ordering/failure, truthful asset wording/removal trigger, and release comment accuracy. Preserve all D1-D8 contracts and unrelated dirty files; do not publish, tag, push, or mutate GitHub/remotes.

truths: Normal no-explicit-config startup safely migrates old-only default state before use
Doctor, demo, and explicit-config invocations never move default legacy config or data
Development deployment validates platform, toolchain, systemctl, and reachable user manager before any migration, build, filesystem, or service mutation
Current docs do not claim the renamed curl install works before a matching herdr-go release asset is published
The release workflow describes the embedded web interface accurately
All D1-D8 rename, migration, service, security, and token contracts remain green

artifacts.substantive: mode-gated default-state migration with explicit behavior tests/seam
mode isolation, dev preflight ordering, asset truthfulness, and release comment assertions
truthful pre-release quick-install availability and usable alternatives
current mode isolation, dev preflight, and renamed-asset availability rules

key_links: CLI mode selection gates migrate_legacy_state before default state is touched
dev-deploy preflight precedes migrate_legacy_dir and every mutation class asserted by the contract test
README and installation guide agree with actual release-asset availability and installer failure behavior
workflow archive comment matches the embedded UI packaging implementation
prohibitions: Do not migrate default legacy state in doctor, demo, or explicit-config modes
Do not mutate anything in dev-deploy before prerequisite checks pass
Do not claim a herdr-go release asset exists or has been smoke-tested
Do not publish, tag, push, or mutate GitHub/remotes
Do not weaken D1-D8 or touch files outside this cell

verify: cargo fmt --all --check && cargo test --quiet && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings && bash -n install.sh && bash -n dev-deploy.sh && bash tests/rename_contract.sh && cd web && npm run bundle && npm run test -- --run

trace.outcome: Isolated default-state migration by CLI mode, made dev deployment preflight mutation-safe, and gated curl-install claims on real renamed-release smoke evidence.
